sentences.grammar_explanation: The phrase "Hello world" is a simple two-word expression. "Hello" is an interjection used as a greeting or to attract attention. "World" is a singular common noun referring to the Earth or the realm of human existence. Together, the phrase functions as a greeting directed at the world at large. There is no verb, making it a nominal or exclamatory phrase rather than a full sentence, though the period at the end treats it as a complete utterance. It is famously known as the traditional first output of beginner programming exercises.

tokens.morphology: Verb, to be, present tense, 3rd person singular

sentences.grammar_explanation: This sentence is a simple declarative sentence in the present tense. It follows the basic Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) structure common in English. 'This' is a demonstrative pronoun acting as the subject. 'Is' is the linking verb (a form of 'to be') in the third person singular present tense, connecting the subject to the predicate. 'A' is an indefinite article modifying the noun 'test'. 'Test' is a singular common noun serving as the predicate nominative (subject complement), describing what 'this' refers to.
